name: ConHex
author: Michail Antonow (2002) / AGP port
description: Michail Antonow's two-layer connection game on a board of 41 cells and 69 placement points. Each turn a player pegs an empty point; when you own at least half of a cell's bordering points you claim that cell permanently. Win by linking your two opposite sides with a chain of cells you own (Red top–bottom, Yellow left–right). The four corner cells belong to both their sides; draws are impossible. Yellow may swap on its first turn (pie rule).
